description_long: This is a concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and pedestrian safety in urban environments. The barrier is rectangular with a sloped top edge and two notched cutouts at the base, likely for forklift handling or drainage. Its surface is painted yellow on one side and off-white on the other, both heavily marked with graffiti and spray-painted tags in various colors. The concrete shows signs of wear, with some chipping and discoloration, especially at the corners and edges. The object is solid and robust, designed to withstand vehicle impacts and prevent unauthorized passage. There are no visible reflective strips or warning signs, but the graffiti and markings are prominent.
description: A solid concrete jersey barrier with yellow and off-white sides, covered in graffiti and showing signs of wear and chipping.
description_view_0: Front view shows the yellow-painted side with black and red graffiti, and visible chipping at the top right corner.
description_view_1: Side profile reveals the thickness of the barrier and the sloped top, with visible concrete texture and some paint marks.
description_view_2: Back view displays the off-white side with large red graffiti and additional black writing, as well as the notched base.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, showing the barrier's thickness and surface texture.
